text.text:
Operations have improved slightly waited in line Sat afternoon for 15 min then after ordering the kiosk said the eta would be 30 min but we waited 10-15 min to receive our order. The texting system is now working to let you know when your order has completed.

On a Saturday afternoon by 2pm they were sold out of at least half of their specialty mochis which was rather disappointing. We were able to try a selection of the ones they had including the Oreo, taro cream cheese, strawberry, and pineapple.

The mochi itself was good, fresh, bouncy, and soft. The fillings were a little more all over the place. Because they don’t add sugar some of the fruit filled mochis were borderline sour and the cream is almost yogurt in flavor than what I would expect for a strawberry or pineapple cream. I would almost prefer for them to cut out the cream and just have the mochi filled with the plain fruit with a compote instead.

I would personally stay away from the mochis with cream cheese. They literally take a slab of cream cheese (1oz) and place it in the middle of the mochi. If they wanted incorporate cream cheese in the mochi I would prefer for it to be chopped and mixed into the other ingredients or have them consider whipping the cream cheese for a lighter texture.

Overall the filling aren’t very sweet but the combinations we tried were lackluster.

I would come back to try their smaller mochi boxes as their mochi is fresh but the big mochi balls are overhyped especially in the context of such a long wait.

text.text:
Found this spot on IG and had to try it since my son and I love mochi.
​The Food:
The product itself is delicious. The "Special" mochi are huge—we tried the Black Sesame Matcha Chocolate and the Taro Cream Cheese Oreo. Both were impressive. The regular Sesame Peanut mochi was a highlight for me personally; it brought back memories of growing up in Singapore.
​The Wait & Service:
Be prepared to wait. We arrived at 1:54 PM and despite the sign warning of a 1-hour wait, it actually took 75 minutes to get our order. The most frustrating part is that they do not text you when your order is ready. You have to keep walking back and forth to the counter to check if your number is up, which is a hassle.
​The Price:
We spent about $30 for the Twin Flavor, 2 specials, and 2 regular mochi. It is on the pricier side, but the portions for the special mochi are substantial.
​Verdict:
Great flavors and texture, but the operations need to catch up to the hype. I probably won't be back unless the wait times are significantly reduced.

text.text: Service was fast and easy. Ordered and paid via kiosk. Mochi itself was soft, smooth, and chewy but it was too thick for me. If it was thinner, it would be perfect! Pure durian was very good if you are durian lovers. Matcha green grape was ok for me. Matcha cream cheese was too thick and heavy for my taste but I also like the crispness, freshness, and juiciness from green grape so much. Both flavors are seasonal.

originalText.text: Service was fast and easy. Ordered and paid via kiosk. Mochi itself was soft, smooth, and chewy but it was too thick for me. If it was thinner, it would be perfect! Pure durian was very good if you are durian lovers. Matcha green grape was ok for me. Matcha cream cheese was too thick and heavy for my taste but I also like the crispness, freshness, and juiciness from green grape so much. Both flavors are seasonal.

text.text:
I ordered 1 sesame peanut mochi and 1 purple rice, taro, meat floss mochi. The mochi itself was chewy and had a satisfying texture. The fillings were tasty as well, but I had a hard time finding the taro. I also opted for rice flour and soybean powder on the outsides which was disappointing because the powders melted into the mochi (probably because they were still semi warm). The slight stickiness of the mochi made it a lil hard to handle with my hands. They were awkward to eat and I ultimately put them back in their cups and ate them with a fork. The one with meat floss was my fav. Hard to find a salty sweet mochi. The giant mochi concept seems gimmicky. I'd prefer if they just sold normal sized ones with the same fillings. They seem to have seasonal flavors like a boba shop which is fun.

I went on a Weds around 5pm and there was no wait. Took about 12 mins to order and receive my mochi. I'd definitely come back again if I had a craving for chinese style mochi.

text.text:
Finally paying a visit after all the hype kinda toned down. No line at all on Friday afternoon, with option to pre-order online. Nonetheless, even if you didn’t, self-order at the kiosk was seamless and quick.

The clean petite venue provided some sitting for your waiting time or even for enjoying your mochi on the spot.

I got one of their specialty mochi, whose price tag was quite steep IMO, but prolly justified by the gigantic size, literally filled the container cup. The mochi skin was perfectly chewy and fully stuffed with right balance of taro paste and cream cheese. I wished the soybean powder coating was thicker but I overall enjoyed it. I wouldn’t disagree with some people saying that the portion was probably bit too much esp with heavy filling, that you might not be able to finish it in one sitting while it’s best enjoyed fresh. So good input for them would be considering smaller size as an option?
